title: Calamophyllum teretifolium
intro: Calamophyllum teretifolium, also known as the 'New Zealand Pepper Tree', is a plant species native to New Zealand that belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calamophyllum teretifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
